choices.text: TCP is connection-oriented, meaning it requires an established connection before data transfer can begin. UDP is connectionless, meaning it does not need an established connection to start sending packets. TCP guarantees delivery of data packets in the order they were sent, while UDP does not ensure ordered delivery. TCP uses flow control mechanisms to adjust data transfer rates to match the receiver's processing speed, whereas UDP sends packets at a constant rate without flow control. TCP employs checksum verification to validate data integrity, while UDP does not use checksum for error-checking. TCP supports retransmission of lost packets, whereas UDP does
